decision-tree.root-question: Is the data crossing the HTTP boundary (DRF view that consumes request.data or returns Response)?

branch.conclusion: Not a serializer concern. Use dataclasses/Pydantic internally; skip this methodology.

branch.question: Direction: input (request body) or output (response body)?
Does the endpoint already have a separate Output serializer in `serializers.py`?

branch.conclusion: Create `EntityCreateRequest` (or `EntityUpdateRequest`) AND `EntityResponse` together. Both inherit from Serializer. Stop sharing one serializer.
Create the Input serializer alongside the existing Output. Set required / allow_blank / write_only explicitly on every field.
Create `EntityResponse(serializers.Serializer)` with an explicit allowlist (no `__all__`, no `exclude`).
Create `EntityResponse` AND ensure the parent queryset is provided by a selector with the correct `select_related` / `prefetch_related`. Nested serializer iterates pre-fetched data only.

example.trace: POST /api/users/ → q1=yes → q2=input → q3=no → r1-input-output-split (create UserCreateRequest + UserResponse)
